description_long: This rifle features a robust, tactical design with a dark metal body and a perforated barrel shroud for heat dissipation. The stock is padded and reinforced, likely for recoil absorption, and is attached to a sturdy receiver. The grip is made of wood or a wood-textured polymer, providing a classic touch to the otherwise modern appearance. There is a visible ammunition belt feeding into the receiver, suggesting it is a belt-fed automatic or semi-automatic weapon. The barrel is long and fitted with a muzzle device, possibly a flash hider or compensator, and the top of the receiver appears to have basic iron sights. The overall finish is matte with a mix of black, olive green, and brown tones, giving it a rugged, utilitarian look suitable for urban combat scenarios.
description: A tactical, belt-fed rifle with a perforated barrel shroud, padded stock, wooden grip, and matte black and green finish. Features a long barrel with a muzzle device and basic iron sights.
description_view_0: The rifle is seen from the rear, highlighting the padded stock and the alignment of the receiver and barrel.
description_view_1: A full side profile reveals the perforated barrel shroud, wooden grip, belt-fed mechanism, and the overall length of the rifle.
description_view_2: The front view emphasizes the muzzle device, barrel opening, and the circular pattern of the barrel shroud.
description_view_3: The opposite side profile shows the same features as view 1, with the belt feed and grip visible from the other side.
